title: Policy Analyst
description: A Policy Analyst in India typically spends their days analyzing data, researching policies, and providing recommendations to government agencies, NGOs, and private organizations. They often work on projects related to social welfare, economic development, and environmental sustainability. Their work involves collaborating with stakeholders, conducting field visits, and presenting findings to clients or policymakers.
degree_name: Master of Arts in Public Policy

career_progression.year_0_2: Typically starts as a Research Assistant or Policy Analyst in a government agency or NGO, working on projects related to policy analysis and research.
career_progression.year_3_5: Moves to a more senior role as a Policy Advisor or Senior Researcher, taking on more complex projects and leading teams.
career_progression.year_6_10: Becomes a Policy Director or Head of Research, overseeing large-scale projects and developing strategic plans.
senior_paths: Head of Research at a Think Tank
Director of Policy at a Private Company
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Can start a consulting firm or a think tank, offering policy analysis and research services to clients.
career_progression.global_pathway: Can work with international organizations such as the World Bank, UNDP, or the European Union, or work with foreign governments on policy projects.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Starting salary is around ₹3-4 lakhs per annum, and the work can be demanding with long hours and tight deadlines.
common_misconceptions: Policy Analysts are only government employees
Policy Analysts are only involved in politics
